rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF OPHELIA C. HILTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Payment Of Fees And Costs To Conservator Of The Person And To Her Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/03/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) The proof of service shows an old address of Caroline Hinshaw whose request for special notice is on file. The address change notice was filed on 07/09/2019. 2) The proof of service shows a different address for Marc Seidenfeld whose request for special notice is on file, but no address change notice has been filed by the requester. 3) When did the petitioner increase her hourly rate to $145? Did the petitioner file a proposed new hourly schedule or statement of compensation? 4) According to the prior fee petition (filed 03/10/2011), the petitioner requested compensation for her services through 03/06/2011 and obtained court approval. Why is the petitioner's time and service log including her services from 08/30/2010 through 02/22/2011? 5) Some dates of the petitioner's visits to the conservatee do not appear on the status reports. *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GRADY LOVETT 2011 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Establish The Equitable Adoption Of Princess Green By Grady Lovett
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/17/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) According to the proof of service (filed 06/16/2020), less than 30-day notice was given to some beneficiaries. 2) Has Ascension Baptist Church's address changed from 1311 Quesada Avenue? 3) The date of death of Sherlyn Brown, together with the personal representative of her estate or successors-in-interest, must be identified. Notice must be given to the personal representative, successors-in-interest, or other person entitled to notice under Probate Code § 17203(c). 4) Direct notice must be given to Andrew Jackson (Bradshaw). The notice to c/o Hedy Russell is insufficient. See CRC 7.51(a)(2). 5) The attorney's supplemental declaration does not explain why the issue of the parent-child relationship based on the equitable adoption doctrine for purposes of property taxation cannot be adjudicated by the San Francisco Assessment Appeals Board without this Court's judicial predetermination. In all cases cited in the memorandum of points and authorities, there were underlying petitions such as Probate Code § 11700 petitions which necessarily required the probate courts' determinations of the parent-child relationships. In this case, however, the petitioner's entitlement to the subject trust property is not in dispute and does not depend on the etablishment of the equitable adoption. *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ODYSSEE WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT
rulings.Rulings: continued to 7/27/2020 the hearing calendar for 7/13/2020 is closed. Issues: 1) The original petition had no accounting attached. The 12/18/17 Supplement has dates 04/26/16 to 07/31/17. The second supplement has dates 07/01/16 to 07/31/17. The first amended second account has dated 07/01/16 to 07/31/18. The accounting needs to be amended as a 2 year accounting to begin 07/20/16 as the prior accounting ended 07/19/16. 2) Prior Examiner Notes Regarding Heirs. On 1/18/17, this Court authorized Petitioner to pay $2,250 to HeirSearch.com to search for Decedent's paternal heirs. On 7/14/17, Petitioner filed a Research Summary by Heirsearch.com [copy in file]. This summary stated that due to challenges in this research it had only been able to identify Decedent's parents and that an additional 20 hours of research would be necessary to identify the paternal heirs. The Petition states that Decedent's mother is Lola Williams. The Research Summary shows that Decedent's mother was Martha James [illegible surname]. Petitioner was asked to explain the discrepancy. Petitioner attached a declaration reciting family ancestry and attaching various documents. However there are no official records showing that Lola was Decedent's mother. If the mother is not Lola Williams, how is James related to Decedent?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AMY J. REECE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/03/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) According to the proof of service (filed 06/22/2020), notice was given before the amended petition was filed. Notice may not be mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. See LRSF 14.21A. 2) The caption of the proposed order must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". See LRSF 14.67(L)(7)(b). 3) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner must be filed to explain why the heirs should be permitted to withdraw their disclaimers in light of Probate Code § 281 providing that a disclaimer, when effective, is irrevocable. *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ENEZ ANITA BESSARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/27/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No courtesy copies of the competing petition and opposition filed by Gwendolyn Sacco have been received by the assigned examiner. 2) No proposed orders have been provided by Gwendolyn Sacco. 3) The beneficiaries of the devisee trust must be clearly identified. See LRSF 14.26(D)(2). 4) The personal representative of the estate of Raymond Bessard or his heirs/devisees must be identified. See LRSF 14.26(E)(2). 5) No duties and liabilities of personal representative form has been filed by Gwendolyn Sacco. 6) The proof of service of notice filed by each petitioner lists different addresses for Deanna Bessard, Giovanna Alexander, and Brianna Alexander. 7) The proof of service filed by Gwendolyn Sacco does not show notice was given to the other petitioner (only her attorney is on the service list). 8) The proof of service filed by Gwendolyn Sacco does not show separate notice was given to Gianna Sacco-Alexander, Giovanna Alexander, and Brianna Alexander. See CRC 7.51(a)(3). 8) No explanation has been provided from Joscelyn Jones Torru as to why the other petitioner should not be appointed as personal representative of the estate. *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.
